column1: # Aquaporin (MIP) family — membrane channels that split by what they conduct.
# All aquaporins share the same six-transmembrane MIP fold with two NPA motifs,
# yet the family divides into two functional classes: the classical water-only
# channels (AQP0/1/2/4/5) and the aquaglyceroporins (AQP3/7/9) that also pass
# glycerol and other small solutes. The inferred tree recovers that split — the
# "_glycerol" rows form their own clade — so, like the globins, the family
# groups by function rather than by anything else. A second membrane-channel
# example to sit alongside the opsin GPCRs, with its own InterProScan MIP-domain
# overlay. No natural reference row, so no `relativeTo`.
